summary: Executed backend pytest regression (5 critical API tests) and frontend Playwright E2E for dashboard, project workspace, tasks, file explorer and OpenClaw offline handling. Core flows pass except Kanban drag-and-drop UI status transition.

ui_bugs.issue: Dragging task card to RUNNING does not move card; status remains unchanged in UI.

integration_issues.issue: Drop interaction triggers PUT /api/tasks with invalid status payload (observed 400 in browser console), so optimistic update rolls back.

action_items: Fix DnD drop target handling in KanbanBoard so status sent to /api/tasks is always one of TODO/RUNNING/DONE.
Remove hardcoded/default OpenClaw URLs and backend proxy base URL; read strictly from environment variables.
Investigate hydration mismatch warnings from dnd-kit aria-describedby IDs in TaskCard/Kanban rendering.
critical_code_review_comments: frontend/lib/openclaw.ts and frontend/app/api/events/route.ts use hardcoded fallback endpoints (localhost:3001), violating env-only configuration rule.
backend/server.py hardcodes FRONTEND_BASE_URL='http://127.0.0.1:3000' instead of environment-based config.
KanbanBoard.handleDragEnd can derive nextStatus from over.id, which may be a task id and not a valid enum, leading to 400 on /api/tasks.
seed_data_creation: Created test projects with TEST_/UI TEST prefixes, tasks, and file knowledge/ui-e2e.md during E2E validation.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Use workspace slug ui-test-1773360116 for quick repro. API-level task status update and file persistence both work; drag-and-drop specifically fails. Offline OpenClaw state and clear 503 error message are validated.
rca of the issue: Repro: create task in TODO, drag to RUNNING. Observed no move and console shows 400 from /api/tasks. Likely root cause is nextStatus fallback to over.id in KanbanBoard; when pointer lands over nested draggable/task element, over.id is task id (not enum), causing schema rejection. Mitigation: resolve destination from droppable container metadata only and guard against invalid enum before API call.
